title: Gas report - Week 14
summary:
LNG
The LNG market softened this week, with declines in the Atlantic basin, while the Pacific market remained relatively stable. On the BLNG1 Gladstone-Tokyo route, 174k cbm vessels edged up by $200, reaching $27,300 per day, while 160k cbm vessels fell $400, closing at $17,400 per day. The slight gain in larger vessel rates suggests a steady demand outlook in the Pacific.

children.title: LNG

On the BLNG1 Gladstone-Tokyo route, 174k cbm vessels edged up by $200, reaching $27,300 per day, while 160k cbm vessels fell $400, closing at $17,400 per day.

children.title: LPG
children.summary: The LPG market maintained its positive momentum this week, with rates rising across key routes. On the BLPG1 Ras Tanura-Chiba route, rates climbed by $4.58, reaching $59.17, while TCE earnings increased by $4,482, closing at $41,720.
